title: Podman kube limits annotation value to 63 bytes instead of k8s 256KB spec
body:
### Issue Description

If value of an annotation is more than 63 characters, `podman kube play` errors out.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a kube yaml
2. Add an annotation, say `io.podman.annotations.infra.name`, with value 64 characters
3. Run `podman kube play <yaml>`


### Describe the results you received

Error: playing YAML file: invalid annotation "io.podman.annotations.infra.name"="abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ012345678901" value length exceeds Kubernetetes max 63

### Describe the results you expected

The command should work and pod should get started.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

Kubernetes has 63 characters limitation on the annotation name and 256KB on the total name+value.
https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/validation/objectmeta.go#L44-L67

It seems Podman is applying 63 characters limit on individual annotation value.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/domain/infra/abi/play.go#L294
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/kube/play.go#L256
